title: Censuring Representative Robert Garcia of California for inciting violence against a special government employee.
plain_summary: This bill is about punishing Representative Robert Garcia from California. The bill says Garcia used bad language about Elon Musk and told Democrats to bring weapons to fight him. Since Musk works for the government, the bill says Garcia was trying to encourage violence against a government worker. The punishment would be a public censure, which means Garcia would be officially criticized in front of everyone in the House of Representatives.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ethics.

sections.title: Resolution Censuring Representative Robert Garcia of California for inciting violence against a special government employee
sections.text: This resolution formally criticizes Representative Robert Garcia of California for encouraging violence against a government worker. The resolution states that Elon Musk is a businessman who works as a special government employee. He is working with President Trump as part of a temporary organization called DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency) to find and stop waste, fraud, and abuse in the federal government. During a hearing on February 12, 2025, about stopping government waste and fraud, Representative Garcia called Elon Musk a bad name. Later that same day on CNN News Central, Representative Garcia told his fellow Democratic Party members to bring actual weapons to this bar fight against Elon Musk. The resolution says that by telling Democrats to bring actual weapons to fight Elon Musk, Representative Garcia was trying to encourage violence against a federal government employee. The resolution also says Democratic Party leaders have not spoken out against Representative Garcia's attempt to encourage violence. The resolution states that violence, threats of violence, or trying to encourage violence against federal employees should not be allowed in the House of Representatives. It says Representative Garcia's actions hurt the dignity and integrity of House proceedings. Therefore, the resolution decides that Representative Garcia should be censured (formally criticized), must come to the front of the House chamber to hear the censure announced, and the Speaker must read this resolution out loud as part of the censure.
